title: Immortal, Invisible, God Only Wise

metadata.composer: 'St. Denio' or 'Joanna' or 'Palestrina' traditional Welsh found in "Caniadau y Cyssegr" by John Roberts, 1839. "Caniadau y Cyssegr a'r Teulu", 1878, alt
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2005 Revision.
c_lines: traditional Welsh found in "Caniadau y Cyssegr" by John Roberts, 1839.
Setting: "Caniadau y Cyssegr a'r Teulu", 1878, alt.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2005 Revision.

1.raw_text: Immortal, invisi- ble, God only wise, In light inaccessible hid from our eyes, Most blessèd, most glorious, the Ancient of Days, Almighty, victorious, Thy great Name we praise.

2.raw_text: Unresting, unhasting, and silent as light, Nor wanting, nor wasting, Thou rulest in might; Thy justice, like mountains, high soaring above Thy clouds, which are fountains of goodness and love.

3.raw_text: To all, life Thou givest, to both great and small; In all life Thou livest, the true life of all; We blossom and flourish as leaves on the tree, And wither and perish but naught changeth Thee.

4.raw_text: Great Father of glory, pure Father of light, Thine angels adore Thee, all veiling their sight; But of Thy rich graces this grace, Lord, impart Take the veil from our face, the vile from our heart.

5.raw_text: All laud we would render; O help us to see 'Tis only the splendor of light hideth Thee, And so let Thy glory, almighty, impart, Through Christ in His story, Thy Christ to the heart.
